business.headline: Gift Deed vs Sale Deed vs Will: Here's how to pick the right way to transfer propertyA single document can decide how smoothly your property changes hands and whether your family faces disputes later. So, should you opt for a Gift Deed, Sale Deed or a Will?

India's biggest private banks are quietly reducing their workforce. Why?HDFC Bank, ICICI Bank and Axis Bank ended FY26 with more than 13,000 fewer employees. The decline reflects attrition, redeployment and technology-led productivity rather than mass layoffs.

Who does Warren Buffett credit for his extraordinary success?Buffett bought his first shares at the age of 11, purchasing three shares of Cities Service for $38 each. That small investment marked the beginning of a career that would eventually turn him into one of the world's most successful investors.

SBI Funds Management lists at nearly 7% premium: Should you buy, sell or hold?SBI Funds Management listed at a modest premium after its heavily subscribed IPO. The debut beat the issue price but fell short of grey market expectations, leaving investors split on booking gains or holding.

Why are farmers marching to Delhi? Key reasons behind the protestThe protest, organised under the banner of the 'Desh Bachao Morcha', will see farmers from Punjab, Haryana, Rajasthan and Himachal Pradesh assemble at Kisan Ghat. Farmer unions say the proposed agreement could hurt India's agriculture sector by opening the door to cheaper American farm products.
Sensex, Nifty open lower as HDFC Bank drags; IT stocks cap lossesThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 54.36 points to 77,654.16, while the NSE Nifty50 slipped 8.70 points to 24,229.80 as of 9:25 am.

SBI Funds Management IPO to list today: Will it open strongly? Check GMPSBI Funds Management will make its market debut on Tuesday after strong IPO demand. Grey market trends and institutional buying have kept expectations of healthy listing gains alive.

Will India raise ethanol blending beyond 20%? Here's what the Minister saysResponding to concerns about vehicle performance, the Minister said the government has not received any widespread or verified complaints from vehicle manufacturers, automobile associations or consumer groups regarding engine failure, fuel pump problems, corrosion or water contamination linked to E20 fuel.

Dalal Street ends lower on weak bank earnings, rising Middle East tensionsThe BSE Sensex fell 442.93 points, or 0.57%, to close at 77,708.52. The NSE Nifty50 also ended in the red, slipping 95.80 points, or 0.39%, to settle at 24,238.50.

Government says no proposal to scrap LTCG tax on equitiesThe government has ruled out scrapping the long-term capital gains tax on listed equities, Finance MoS Pankaj Chaudhary told the Lok Sabha. The government also said LTCG tax collections rose 78% year-on-year, highlighting its growing contribution to revenue.

Rising crude prices hit fuel margins. Why are OMC profits still holding up?Despite weaker fuel marketing margins, integrated profits, which combine refining and fuel marketing performance, remained well above recent averages.

SBI Funds Management IPO lists tomorrow with 18% GMP: Is it a long-term buy?SBI Funds Management is all set to debut on the stock exchanges tomorrow, backed by a strong 18% grey market premium. But does that make it a good long-term investment? Let's have a look.

EPFO pension stopped? Submit Digital Life Certificate from home in 6 easy stepsHas your EPFO pension stopped unexpectedly? One reason could be that your Digital Life Certificate (Jeevan Pramaan) has not been submitted. Here's how to complete the process from home in six easy steps.
Why has Apple increased music subscription prices in India?Apple Music users in India will now have to pay more for their subscriptions. Why has the company decided to increase prices now?

HDFC Bank, Axis Bank fall up to 6%: Why are bank stocks falling today?At around 12.55 pm, HDFC Bank was down 5.1% at Rs 778, while Axis Bank had plunged 5.7% to Rs 1,252.80. Kotak Mahindra Bank also fell 2.6% to Rs 379.85, pulling the Nifty Bank down 883.40 points, or 1.51%, to 57,638.

Accenture has a new way of giving raises. Here's how it worksUnder the new approach, 50% of an approved salary increase will be added to an employee's base salary. The remaining 50% will be paid separately as a one-time lump-sum amount.

Sensex tumbles over 600 points: Why is the stock market falling today?At around 12.48 pm, the BSE Sensex was trading at 77,399.31, down 752.14 points (0.96%), while the Nifty50 fell 193.50 points (0.8%) to 24,140.80.

Why the government is cracking down on restaurant service charges nowThe government has stepped up action against restaurants that add service charges by default. The move signals stricter enforcement after court backing and consumer complaints.

Axis Bank appoints ex-Bandhan Bank finance chief Rajeev Mantri as CFOMantri will assume charge on September 28, 2026, subject to the necessary regulatory and statutory approvals, the bank said in a regulatory filing.

JPMorgan just posted record profit. How big is the world's biggest bank, really?JPMorgan Chase posted a record second-quarter 2026 profit as revenue rose across every business line. The results renewed focus on the bank's vast scale, from market value to daily payment flows.

entertainment.headline: Yeh Awarapan: Arijit Singh, Emraan Hashmi back to relive love and loss in new song Yeh Awarapan, the second song from actor Emraan Hashmi's Awarapan 2, shows his character Shivam Pandit battling grief and heartbreak. The emotional track also marks singer Arijit Singh's return to playback singing after his retirement announcement.

Harshad Chopda shows middle finger to Shreya, internet calls him manchild A Lock Upp promo shows Harshad Chopda making an obscene gesture at co-contestant Shreya Kalra during a food-related argument. The clip has renewed focus on their long-running friction inside the house.

Richie Mehta's Phoolan to premiere at Toronto Film Festival ahead of OTT release Prime Video's Phoolan will screen in the Special Presentations section at the 2026 Toronto International Film Festival. The selection takes a defining chapter from Phoolan Devi's life to a global festival audience.

Can Jana Nayagan balance Vijay the superstar and Vijay the Chief Minister? Jana Nayagan arrives as Vijay's farewell film while openly embracing his public role as Tamil Nadu's Chief Minister. Its central challenge is balancing star-driven entertainment with direct political messaging.

Jennifer Winget's bridal gown stitched her love story into the fabric. See pics Jennifer Winget has posted fresh photographs from her intimate UK wedding to William Ishmael. The images have drawn attention to her detailed bridal ensemble and the couple's understated ceremony.

Homecoming has been toughest: Amitabh Bachchan shares health update after surgery Amitabh Bachchan said in a new blog post that returning home after surgery has been the hardest part of his recovery. His note about the physical and psychological strain drew concern from fans and highlighted the challenges of recuperation.

Jana Nayagan hits Rs 15 crore in advance booking, but still behind Leo and GOAT Jana Nayagan has posted strong advance bookings despite being leaked online before release. The trend suggests the piracy scare has not weakened audience interest in Vijay's first film as Chief Minister.

Toxic's new song Madhosh drips with Yash, Tara Sutaria's steamy chemistry. Watch The makers of Toxic have unveiled Madhosh, the film's second single, featuring Yash and Tara Sutaria in a poignant romantic number. The video also includes brief appearances by Nayanthara and Kiara Advani, adding to the intrigue surrounding the film.

Actor Siddharth breaks down as martyr's wife thanks Operation Safed Sagar team At the Mumbai trailer launch of Operation Safed Sagar, Siddharth broke down after Alka Ahuja thanked the team. The exchange underscored the care taken to portray Squadron Leader Ajay Ahuja with integrity.

After National Award win, Kartik Aaryan offers prayers at Mahakaleshwar. Videos Days after winning his first National Award, Kartik Aaryan visited the Mahakaleshwar Temple in Ujjain with his parents and took part in the aarti.

Rang De Basanti to Yuva: Bollywood films that showed dissent isn't anti-national As young people gather at Delhi's Jantar Mantar for the Cockroach Janta Party protest, Hindi cinema offers a timely mirror. Films like Rang De Basanti, Yuva, No One Killed Jessica, Sardar Udham and Main Azaad Hoon have long portrayed protest as an essential part of a vibrant democracy.

Jaaved Jaaferi reveals he was considered for Life in a... Metro, Munna Bhai MBBS Actor Jaaved Jaaferi recalled coming close to key roles in Life in a... Metro, Munna Bhai MBBS and Parinda. He said the near misses stayed with him, but he accepted them as fate.

Director Sandeep Reddy Vanga shared Prabhas Spirit update at US event. Sandeep Reddy Vanga said at the TTA convention that nearly 40 per cent of Spirit has been shot. The update reinforces the film's March 5, 2027 release plan after recent delay rumours.

Farhan Akhtar sings Meri Laundry at wedding, gets rinsed by the internet Actors Farhan Akhtar and Shibani Dandekar were trolled after a wedding performance video went viral. The clip drew criticism over their on-stage coordination while Farhan remains in focus over the Don 3 controversy.

Actor Karan Patel on OTT vs television: Daily soaps have lost their importance Actor Karan Patel said OTT and binge-watching have weakened the hold daily soaps once had on family routines. He also revisited Yeh Hai Mohabbatein's legacy and said its long-form storytelling cannot be easily rebooted for the web.

Did they sell their soul?: Anurag Kashyap slams Delhi police action against students Protest over education irregularities and the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak in Delhi turned violent, leading to police lathi charge and teargas, sparking widespread criticism. Filmmaker Anurag Kashyap condemned the Delhi Police's action, questioning their moral stance.

History won't forgive it: Prakash Raj to celebs silent on CJP's students protest Prakash Raj used the CJP's Chalo Sansad march to question the film industry's silence on the student-led protests in New Delhi. He said such moments reveal who stands with people and warned history would judge those who stayed quiet.

Rang De Basanti scene goes viral amid CJP protest. Here's why A Rang De Basanti scene is trending on social media amid nationwide protest over NEET irregularities and the demand for education reforms. The iconic dialogue on change and responsibility has resonated with many social media users online.

Exclusive | TXT's Yeonjun on the 'all-rounder' tag, NO LABELS: PART 02, Indian fans In an exclusive interview with India Today, TXT's Yeonjun reflects on NO LABELS: PART 02, balancing perfection with vulnerability and finding his own artistic identity. He also shares his thoughts on Indian fans.

general news.headline: Big win for Sonam Wangchuk, court says he can be moved to Medanta hospitalAfter climate activist Sonam Wangchuk, who has been on a hunger strike for 20 days, was forcibly removed from Delhi's Jantar Mantar and shifted to Safdarjung Hospital, the Delhi High Court on Tuesday allowed his transfer to Medanta Hospital, directing that he continue to receive treatment under a panel of doctors at the private facility.

Did govt and Delhi Police misread scale of Cockroach Janta Party protest?Despite weeks of prior signals, the Delhi Police and the government seem to have failed to accurately estimate the number of protesters who would gather for the march to Parliament on Monday. As the crowd swelled and reached the Parliament doorsteps, the police were clearly caught off guard.

She kissed me without consent: IPS trainee accused of harassment attempts suicideA case was registered against Uday Krishna Reddy after a fellow IPS trainee at the National Police Academy accused him of stalking, assault, harassment and secretly recording a private video.

Why are farmers marching to Delhi? Key reasons behind the protestThe protest, organised under the banner of the 'Desh Bachao Morcha', will see farmers from Punjab, Haryana, Rajasthan and Himachal Pradesh assemble at Kisan Ghat. Farmer unions say the proposed agreement could hurt India's agriculture sector by opening the door to cheaper American farm products.

How anti-Taliban fighters seized Afghan district on Pakistan bordersAn armed anti-Taliban group claimed it briefly seized the Yaftal-e-Paeen district headquarters in Afghanistan's Badakhshan for hours before withdrawing. While the Taliban said it quickly restored control, if verified, it would mark the first known instance since it returned to power in 2021 when an Afghan district headquarters briefly slipped from its control.

Un-Indian, PM must apologise: Rahul Gandhi on cops beating students at CJP stirCongress leader Rahul Gandhi has questioned Prime Minister Narendra Modi's silence on the police crackdown on students at the CJP protest march, while demanding that he apologises for the act.

NEET paper leak a grave sin, can't happen again: PM Modi as protests rageAt the NDA Parliamentary Party meeting, Prime Minister Narendra Modi termed the NEET paper leak a "ghor paap", said 13 accused have been arrested, and urged strict action and state cooperation to prevent future leaks.

PV Sindhu's redemption story began with a phone call to Virat KohliPV Sindhu didn't rebuild her career to win the Japan Open. She rebuilt it to rediscover joy. In Tokyo, beating Akane Yamaguchi for her maiden Super 750 title, she found both. And it all began with a conversation with Virat Kohli.

Engineering from Chaitanya Bharathi and no IIT, Uday Ruddarraju is now OpenAI CTOOpenAI has a new CTO and he is Uday Ruddarraju with his Hyderabad roots. Interestingly, Uday is not from any IIT or a similarly storied institute. Instead, he finished his engineering from Chaitanya Bharathi Institute of Technology.

Colder, taller, deeper: Storm clouds cover 70% of India, Isro satellites showIsro's INSAT-3DR and INSAT-3DS satellites show cloud cover crossing 70 per cent of India on July 21, 2026, with storm tops turning colder and taller. Here is what Cloud Top Brightness Temperature reveals about the storms overhead, and what it means for Delhi.

Kerala sailor killed in Russian ship attack was only child, got engaged 3 months agoAkhil Joyan, 26, was among four Indians who were killed on Sunday after a Russian attack on a cargo ship as it was departing Ukraine's Port of Odesa.

Why Indian students are ditching the US, flocking to European universitiesFor decades, the US was the dream destination for Indian students. But rising costs, tougher visa rules under the second Donald Trump administration and a new India-EU mobility framework are prompting many to look towards Europe. European universities are offering cheaper education, clearer post-study pathways and greater certainty.

Viral video: Tourists walk on Yellowstone's rainbow. It is alive, and can kill youTourists were filmed walking in a restricted area of Yellowstone's Grand Prismatic Spring, and the video has been viewed millions of times. Here is the science of why the rainbow colours are made of living bacteria, and why a single footprint can scar the spring for decades.

Live: Sonam Wangchuk's wife hails court order to move him to private hospitalCJP Protest in Delhi Live: A day after the Cockroach Janta Party's 'Chalo Sansad' march turned violent, political leaders have been visiting those injured in the clashes. Union Health Minister JP Nadda visited Delhi's Ram Manohar Lohia (RML) Hospital and Lady Hardinge Medical College and Hospital, while Congress leaders Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra met injured students at RML Hospital. Several protesters and police personnel were injured in Monday's clashes between demonstrators and security forces. Security remains heightened across the national capital. Follow India Today for the latest updates.

Russian woman tries Rs 97 face massage at Delhi men's salon. Indians warn herA Russian woman entered a men's salon in Delhi and tried a face massage during her walk through the city. The video of the Rs 96.47 treatment went viral, with many urging her to stay safe.

Gift Deed vs Sale Deed vs Will: Here's how to pick the right way to transfer propertyA single document can decide how smoothly your property changes hands and whether your family faces disputes later. So, should you opt for a Gift Deed, Sale Deed or a Will?
The protest before the CJP protest: Gen Z's fight for permission at homeFor many Gen Zers, the road to the Jantar Mantar protest began with a family argument.

Live: Punjab farmers en route to Delhi stopped at Khanauri, Shambhu bordersFarmers from Punjab, Haryana and several other states are heading to Delhi for a Mahapanchayat at Kisan Ghat against the proposed India-US trade deal. Haryana Police have stepped up security at key border points, stopping farmer convoys at Khanauri and Bastara while maintaining heavy barricading at the Shambhu border. Prohibitory orders under Section 163 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita (BNS) remain in force in parts of Haryana. Follow India Today for the latest live updates.

Publishing Mamata-era report, BJP joins four-way tug-of-war over Shahid DiwasFor decades, the July 21 Shahid Diwas or Martyrs' Day was the Trinamool Congress's biggest annual show of strength. This year, the Shahid Diwas has turned into a four-way tug-of-war. The three TMC camps and the Congress are staking claim, while the Bengal BJP government has joined in by releasing a long-pending report.

Govt summons Russian envoy after 4 Indians die in merchant ship attack in UkraineIndia summoned the Russian envoy and lodged a strong protest over the killing of four Indian crew members in the alleged Russian missile strike on the cargo vessel off Ukraine's Odesa coast.

Will CM Vijay take up airports, sewage challenge thrown by Niti report?The Niti Aayog's first-ever Investment Friendliness Index ranks Tamil Nadu third in India and praises its strengths. But it also flags areas such as airport capacity, treated sewage water, and port connectivity as key gaps. The challenge now is whether CM Vijay can turn these shortcomings into opportunities and make Tamil Nadu India's most investment-friendly state.

Behind the barricades: Inside Parliament as CJP protest shook DelhiThe opening day of Parliament unfolded as a tense parallel contest. Massive protests and heightened security outside, while inside the government and Opposition pursued strategy, negotiations and political messaging on a day of disruption and uncertainty.

This Indian city ranks sixth in world's happiest cities 2026 listOne heritage city from India has secured a place on Time Out's world's happiest cities 2026 list and no, it's probably not the one you're thinking about.

Video: CM Vijay's surprise inspection of India's first bio-CNG plant in ChennaiTamil Nadu Chief Minister Vijay made a surprise visit to India's first bio-CNG plant in Chennai. He used the inspection to press for stronger scientific waste management and wider replication of such projects across Tamil Nadu.

sports.headline: India favourites to win T20I series vs Zimbabwe? Sikandar Raza does not think so ZIM vs IND: Zimbabwe captain Sikandar Raza has rejected the notion that India are overwhelming favourites for the upcoming T20I series, saying both teams have an equal chance of winning.

Hockey: India name World Cup squad with a good mix of youth and experience Hockey India have named a 20-member men's World Cup squad led by Harmanpreet Singh, with youth and experience across all lines. India have been drawn alongside Wales, England and Pakistan in the group stages of the tournament, starting August 15.

Want cheap Australian Open tickets? You may have to act faster in 2027 The Australian Open will cap allocations of its popular A$49 ground passes during the opening week of the 2027 tournament after crowding and long queues marred this year's event in Melbourne.

Gambhir bashed for KL Rahul call: Like Samson, Sooryavanshi, you screwed him up Krishnamachari Srikkanth criticised Gautam Gambhir and the team management for sending KL Rahul in at No. 6 during India's chase at Lord's. His remarks have revived questions over Rahul's role after India lost the ODI series to England.

Falklands banner backlash: Do Argentina stars face a Premier League ban? Argentina players sparked a row after displaying a Falklands banner following their World Cup win over England. However, any move to revoke Premier League work visas would be highly unlikely.

Thank You, Lionel Messi, for giving the world one last bit of magic Lionel Messi may have possibly signed off from the international football like a veteran magician leaving his stage for the final time. During the last two World Cup campaigns, Messi showed how he was able to galvanise the Argentina team for a common goal.

Who needs trophies? Loyal Argentina fans give hero's welcome to losing finalists Argentina's beaten World Cup finalists arrived in Buenos Aires on Monday. Thousands greeted them with flares, drums and chants, underscoring loyalty that outlasted the missing trophy.

Pat Cummins returns as Australia pick full-strength squad for Bangladesh Tests AUS vs BAN: Pat Cummins, Josh Hazlewood and Nathan Lyon have returned to Australia's 13-man squad for the two-Test World Test Championship series against Bangladesh in Darwin and Mackay.

Inside Spain's World Cup party in Madrid: Footballers turn DJs for 2 million fans Spain's FIFA World Cup winners turned Madrid's Cibeles celebration into a giant DJ-led street party. The homecoming fused state ceremony, football folklore and viral fan moments before nearly two million supporters.

Have FIFA World Cup finals become boring this century? Spain's 1-0 extra-time victory over Argentina brought the curtain down on another unforgettable World Cup. Yet after four weeks of drama, goals and upsets, the final once again struggled to match the spectacle that came before it. Has football's biggest match become the most boring?

PV Sindhu's redemption story began with a phone call to Virat Kohli PV Sindhu didn't rebuild her career to win the Japan Open. She rebuilt it to rediscover joy. In Tokyo, beating Akane Yamaguchi for her maiden Super 750 title, she found both. And it all began with a conversation with Virat Kohli.

UTT Season 7: Sathiyan steers Dabang Delhi to crucial win over Ahmedabad APL PipersUTT Season 7: Sathiyan Gnanasekaran guided Dabang Delhi TTC to a 9-6 win over Ahmedabad APL Pipers with victories in the singles and mixed doubles. The result gave Delhi their third win of the campaign and kept them in the playoff race.

Pain is immense: Messi breaks silence after heartbreaking World Cup final defeatLionel Messi has spoken for the first time since Argentina's heartbreaking defeat to Spain in the FIFA World Cup final, admitting the pain of losing the title was "immense" while expressing pride in his team's remarkable run to a second successive final.

FIFA World Cup: Yamal consoled Messi after final, hugs him; moment goes viralWhile emotions ran high after the FIFA World Cup final, one post-match moment stood out above the chaos. As several Argentina and Spain players were involved in heated confrontations following the final whistle, including one Argentine player being sent off, Spain's teenage star Lamine Yamal shared a warm embrace with Lionel Messi. The heartfelt exchange, captured on camera, quickly went viral on social media, with many fans describing it as a symbolic passing of the torch from one generation to the next. Yamal was also seen consoling the Argentine captain in the aftermath of the dramatic contest, offering a striking contrast to the tensions unfolding elsewhere on the pitch. For many viewers, the brief interaction became one of the defining images of the final, highlighting mutual respect and sportsmanship despite the fierce rivalry.

Arjun Erigaisi beats world champion Gukesh with black to jump to joint leadIndia's Arjun Erigaisi outplayed world champion D Gukesh to move into the joint lead after the fifth round of the fourth edition of the Chennai Grand Masters on Monday.

Messi's tears suggest World Cup final was his last for Argentina: Baichung BhutiaIndian football legend Bhaichung Bhutia said he was "sad to see Messi cry" after Argentina's World Cup final defeat to Spain, adding that the tournament could mark the end of Lionel Messi's international career. In an exclusive conversation with Rajdeep Sardesai, Bhutia reflected on Messi's legacy, Argentina's campaign, Spain's World Cup triumph and what the Argentine great has meant to football across generations.

England legend and Liverpool icon Kevin Keegan dies at 75Former Liverpool and England star Kevin Keegan has died at the age of 75 following a battle with cancer. Keegan made 323 appearances for Liverpool between 1971 and 1977, scoring 100 goals.

FIFA Rankings: Spain reclaim No. 1 spot after World Cup triumph, Argentina secondWorld Cup champions Spain returned to the summit of the FIFA men's rankings on Monday, reclaiming the No. 1 spot after their 1-0 extra-time victory over Argentina in the 2026 World Cup final.

UEFA president boycotts World Cup final in protest against FIFA over Balogun sagaUEFA president Aleksander Ceferin stayed away from the FIFA World Cup final as tensions with world football's governing body escalated over the controversial suspension of Folarin Balogun's one-match ban.

India spends over Rs 53 crore on preparations ahead of Glasgow GamesThe Sports Ministry has spent over Rs 53 crore on preparing Indian athletes for the Glasgow Commonwealth Games, with athletics, boxing and weightlifting receiving the biggest share of the funding.

Rudi Garcia steps down as Belgium coach after World Cup quarterfinal exitBelgium parted ways with head coach Rudi Garcia, the Royal Belgian Football Association (RBFA) announced on Monday. The Frenchman guided the Red Devils to the quarter-finals of the World Cup, where they lost to eventual champions Spain.

PV Sindhu leads India's challenge at China Open after historic Japan Open triumphFresh off her historic Japan Open triumph, PV Sindhu will lead India's campaign at the China Open Super 1000 tournament as she looks to carry her winning momentum into Changzhou.

Sindhu's Japan Open triumph no surprise for Saina: Never write a champion offSaina Nehwal said PV Sindhu's Japan Open title shows she remains a serious contender on the tour. Sindhu, 31, won her first title since Decmeber 2024, beating Akane Yamaguchi in the final on Sunday in Tokyo.

Paula Badosa suffers major heartbreak, forced to retire from Iasi Open finalPaula Badosa's resurgence suffered a heartbreaking setback after the Spaniard retired from the Iasi Open final against Mayar Sherif while trailing 6-4, 4-0, ending her nine-match winning streak.

Rohit Sharma's Lord's hundred put an end to retirement speculation: Saba KarimSaba Karim said Rohit Sharma's Lord's century ended the retirement speculation surrounding the veteran opener, adding that India's priority now is settling their pace attack ahead of the New Zealand tour.

Shubman Gill frustrated with India's injury setbacks, sends strong message.Shubman Gill has raised concerns over India's growing injury problems, questioning the team's fitness standards ahead of the 2027 ODI World Cup. The India captain said repeated setbacks forced the team management to constantly alter combinations during the England series.

Tennis body mandates one-time eligibility test for female playersThe WTA has introduced a new rule requiring female players to undergo a one-time SRY gene test to confirm their eligibility to compete on the women's tour. The policy, which comes into effect on Tuesday, replaces the organisation's previous transgender participation regulations.

Argentina's obsession with dark arts finally backfires in World Cup finalArgentina's trademark intensity delivered trophies for five years, but it deserted them when it mattered most. As Spain lifted the World Cup, Lionel Messi was left watching a final that never gave him the chance to shape the game on football's biggest stage.

Back Rohit Sharma, Virat Kohli or move on: Yuvraj Singh's strong message to BCCIYuvraj Singh has urged the BCCI and team management to give Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli clarity over their ODI futures, saying the selectors must clearly communicate whether the veteran duo remain part of India's plans for the 2027 World Cup.

Great to cheap? Messi under fire for shocking attempt to get Cucurella sent offLionel Messi faces fierce criticism for a cynical attempt to get Spain's Marc Cucurella sent off during the World Cup final on Sunday. Argentina's defeat ended in an ugly post-match brawl.

Watch: Carlos Alcaraz, Rafael Nadal erupt in joy after Spain's World Cup gloryCarlos Alcaraz and Rafael Nadal celebrated on social media after Spain beat Argentina in the FIFA World Cup final. Ferran Torres's extra-time winner capped Spain's title run and triggered jubilation across the country.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
